---
title: "Chapter 17 - Shraddha-Traya Vibhaga Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">श्रद्धात्रयविभागयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-16.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-18.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-17-1" data-reference="17.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-1">17.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, abandoning the injunctions of the scriptures, perform worship with faith—what is their basis, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>: <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, or <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।<br>
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-2" data-reference="17.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-2">17.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Faith embodied beings possess is of three kinds, born of their own nature: <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sāttvika</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rājasika</span>, and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tāmasika</span>. Hear of them from me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा ।<br>
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-3" data-reference="17.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-3">17.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, everyone’s faith is in accordance with their nature. A person is made of faith; whatever his faith is, that is what he is.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत ।<br>
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-4" data-reference="17.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-4">17.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> worship the devas; those in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> worship the yakshas and rakshasas; and others, who are in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, worship the departed and the hosts of spirits.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः ।<br>
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-5" data-reference="17.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-5">17.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who perform severe austerities not enjoined by scripture, being joined with ostentation and egoism, and driven by desire, attachment, and force.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः ।<br>
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-6" data-reference="17.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-6">17.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, being devoid of discernment, torment the body’s collection of beings and Me too, dwelling within the body—know them to be of demonic resolve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः ।<br>
मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-7" data-reference="17.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-7">17.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Food, too, dear to all, is of three kinds. Hear this distinction among <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." aria-label="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." title="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." data-glossary-id="yajna">yajna</span>, austerity, and charity.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः ।<br>
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-8" data-reference="17.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-8">17.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Foods that are juicy, nourishing, stable, and pleasing to the heart are dear to those in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span>; they increase life, purity, strength, health, happiness, and delight.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः ।<br>
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-9" data-reference="17.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-9">17.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Bitter, sour, salty, excessively hot, pungent, dry, and burning foods are preferred by those of rajasic nature; they bring pain, sorrow, and disease.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः ।<br>
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-10" data-reference="17.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-10">17.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Food that is stale, tasteless, foul-smelling, spoiled, and also leftovers, and impure—this is dear to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् ।<br>
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-11" data-reference="17.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-11">17.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">That <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." aria-label="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." title="yajna: Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action." data-glossary-id="yajna">yajna</span> which is offered in accordance with scriptural injunction by those who seek no reward, with the mind firmly resolved that it ought simply to be performed, is sattvic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते ।<br>
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-12" data-reference="17.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-12">17.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But whatever is offered with an aim for the fruit, and indeed for ostentation as well, know that sacrifice to be rajasic, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् ।<br>
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-13" data-reference="17.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-13">17.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Sacrifice performed without regard to proper procedure, without distribution of food, without mantras, without gifts, and devoid of faith is said to be tamasic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् ।<br>
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-14" data-reference="17.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-14">17.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Worship of the devas, brahmins, teachers, and the wise, purity, straightforwardness, celibacy, and nonviolence—these are said to be bodily austerity.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् ।<br>
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-15" data-reference="17.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-15">17.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Speech that causes no agitation, is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial, and also the regular recitation and study of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>—this is called austerity of speech.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् ।<br>
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-16" data-reference="17.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-16">17.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, and self-restraint: this is called mental austerity.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मनः प्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः ।<br>
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-17" data-reference="17.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-17">17.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The threefold austerity performed by men with supreme faith, by those who are steadfast and seek no reward, is called sattvic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः ।<br>
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-18" data-reference="17.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-18">17.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever austerity is performed here for the sake of honor, respect, and worship, and indeed with hypocrisy—that is said to be rajasic, unstable, and impermanent.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् ।<br>
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-19" data-reference="17.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-19">17.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Austerity that is performed with foolish obstinacy, by self-torment, or for the purpose of harming another, is said to be tamasic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः ।<br>
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-20" data-reference="17.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-20">17.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A gift given as a duty, without expectation of return, at the proper place and time, and to a worthy recipient, is regarded as sattvic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे ।<br>
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-21" data-reference="17.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-21">17.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But whatever is given with expectation of return, or with a desire for reward, and given grudgingly, that gift is regarded as rajasic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः ।<br>
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-22" data-reference="17.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-22">17.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A gift given at the wrong place and time, and to unworthy recipients, given without respect and with contempt, is said to be tamasic.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते ।<br>
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-23" data-reference="17.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-23">17.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>, Tat, Sat—this has been declared as the threefold designation of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>. By that the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." aria-label="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." title="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." data-glossary-id="brahmin">brāhmaṇas</span>, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, and sacrifices were ordained in ancient times.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ॐतत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः ।<br>
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-24" data-reference="17.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-24">17.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore, the acts of sacrifice, gift-giving, and austerity, as prescribed by the injunctions, are always undertaken by the expounders of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, uttering <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः ।<br>
प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-25" data-reference="17.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-25">17.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Saying “tat,” without aiming at the fruit, acts of sacrifice, austerity, and various acts of giving are performed by those who desire liberation.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः ।<br>
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-26" data-reference="17.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-26">17.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The word sat is used in the sense of being and in the sense of goodness; and, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, the word sat is likewise applied to a praiseworthy action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते ।<br>
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-27" data-reference="17.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-27">17.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and charity is also called satt; and action performed for that purpose is likewise called satt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते ।<br>
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-28" data-reference="17.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-28">17.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever is offered, given, or practiced in austerity without faith, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Pārtha</span>, is called asat; it is neither here nor after death.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् ।<br>
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
